## Step 1: Define your research question

_Welcome to "Academic Writing with Copilot: Co-evolve Your Research Judgment"! :wave:_

A strong research question is the foundation of any academic paper. Before you can use AI effectively, you need to be clear about **what you are trying to find out**, **why it matters**, and **what you expect to find**. AI can help you expand, refine, and pressure-test your thinking — but the initial intellectual commitment must be yours.

**Why articulate first?**: When you put your question into words before involving AI, you make your own understanding explicit. You'll be able to judge whether AI suggestions are improving your thinking or merely rephrasing it.

### :keyboard: Activity: Write your research question

1. Open a new browser tab and navigate to your newly made repository (your copy of this exercise). Then, work on the steps in your second tab while you read the instructions in this tab.

2. Create a new branch named `academic-writing` from `main`.

   - Navigate to the **< > Code** tab in the header menu of your repository.
   - Click on the **main** branch drop-down.
   - In the text box, enter `academic-writing` and click **Create branch: `academic-writing` from main**.

3. On your new `academic-writing` branch, create a new file named `research-question.md` in the root of the repository.

4. Fill in the file using the template below. Replace every `[...]` placeholder with your own content. You may use a real or hypothetical research topic.

   ```markdown
   # My Research Question

   ## Research Question
   [Write your research question as a single, focused question.]

   ## Hypothesis
   [State what you expect to find and why.]

   ## Key Claims
   - [Claim 1]
   - [Claim 2]
   - [Claim 3]

   ## Why It Matters
   [Explain the significance of this research in 2–3 sentences.]
   ```

5. Commit the file to the `academic-writing` branch with the message `Add research question`.

6. Mona will check your work and post the next step in the issue. Give her a moment and refresh the page.

> **Tip 💡**: Not sure where to start? Ask Copilot: *"Help me turn this topic into a focused research question: [your topic]."* Then evaluate whether its version is sharper or vaguer than yours — and decide which to keep.

<details>
<summary>Having trouble? 🤷</summary><br/>

If you don't get feedback, here are some things to check:
- Make sure the branch is named exactly `academic-writing`.
- Make sure the file is named exactly `research-question.md` and is in the root folder (not in a subfolder).
- Make sure you committed the file to the `academic-writing` branch, not `main`.

</details>
